seoTitle: Capxon Aluminum Electrolytic Capacitors Product Catalog | BeiLuo Electronics
seoDescription: Browse Capxon aluminum electrolytic capacitors, radial lead, snap-in, SMD, and automotive capacitors. Technical specifications, selection guide, and alternative parts.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Capxon capacitor for my application?
faqs.answer: Consider voltage rating, capacitance value, ripple current requirements, temperature range, and lifetime requirements. Capxon offers comprehensive selection guides for each product series. For consumer electronics, radial lead capacitors are typically suitable. For industrial power supplies, consider snap-in types. For automotive applications, select AEC-Q200 qualified automotive series. For compact designs, SMD capacitors are ideal.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your application specifications for personalized Capxon capacitor selection assistance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Capxon's radial and snap-in capacitors?
faqs.answer: Radial capacitors have wire leads for through-hole mounting and are suitable for lower power applications typically found in consumer electronics. Snap-in capacitors have PCB mounting terminals, higher capacitance values, and are designed for high-power applications such as industrial power supplies and inverters. Snap-in types offer better thermal performance and higher ripple current capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use radial capacitors for consumer electronics and low-power applications. Use snap-in capacitors for power supplies above 100W.

faqs.question: What are Capxon's automotive-grade capacitors?
faqs.answer: Capxon's automotive-grade capacitors are specifically designed and tested to meet AEC-Q200 standards for automotive applications. These capacitors undergo rigorous qualification testing including temperature cycling, vibration, and humidity resistance. They feature extended temperature ratings up to 150°C, enhanced reliability, and long operational lifetime. Automotive series are ideal for EV charging systems, onboard electronics, and LED lighting in vehicles.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications requiring AEC-Q200 qualification, select Capxon's automotive series capacitors.

faqs.question: Are Capxon capacitors RoHS compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Capxon capacitors are RoHS compliant and free from lead, mercury, cadmium, and other restricted substances. The company maintains ISO 14001 environmental management certification and is committed to environmentally responsible manufacturing. Capxon capacitors meet international environmental standards and are suitable for worldwide distribution.
faqs.decisionGuide: For RoHS compliance documentation, contact our sales team with your specific part numbers.

faqs.question: What is the lead time for Capxon capacitors?
faqs.answer: Standard Capxon capacitors typically have 4-6 weeks lead time from Taiwan. High-volume orders or custom specifications may require 8-10 weeks. BeiLuo Electronics maintains local stock of popular series for immediate delivery. For urgent requirements, please contact our sales team to check current inventory availability.
faqs.decisionGuide: For urgent requirements, check our stock availability or discuss scheduled orders with our sales team.

categories.name: Radial Lead Capacitors
categories.description: Standard radial aluminum electrolytic capacitors for general-purpose applications
categories.longDescription: Capxon radial lead capacitors are high-reliability aluminum electrolytic capacitors designed for industrial and consumer applications. These capacitors feature wire leads for through-hole mounting and are available in a wide range of capacitance and voltage ratings. The radial series includes standard, low impedance, and high-temperature options to meet various application requirements. Ideal for power supplies, industrial controls, and consumer electronics where reliability is important. Contact BeiLuo Electronics, your authorized Capxon distributor, for radial capacitor selection guidance.
categories.selectionGuide: Select based on capacitance, voltage rating, size constraints, and lifetime requirements. KF series for standard, KM for low impedance, KT for high temperature.

faqs.question: What are the typical applications for Capxon radial capacitors?
faqs.answer: Radial capacitors are used in consumer electronics, power supplies, audio equipment, and industrial controls. They are ideal for applications requiring compact size and reliable performance. The through-hole mounting makes them suitable for both manual and automated assembly processes.
faqs.decisionGuide: For general-purpose applications with moderate capacitance requirements, radial capacitors offer excellent reliability.

faqs.question: What is the difference between KF, KM, and KT series?
faqs.answer: KF series is the standard radial capacitor for general applications. KM series features low impedance for high-frequency applications. KT series offers higher temperature ratings up to 105°C and longer lifetime for demanding environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use KF series for standard applications. Use KM series for switching power supplies. Use KT series for high-temperature applications.

faqs.question: What is the typical lifetime of Capxon radial capacitors?
faqs.answer: Standard radial capacitors are rated for 2,000 to 5,000 hours at 85°C or 105°C. Lifetime can be extended significantly with proper derating. High-temperature KT series offers up to 10,000 hours at 105°C.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended lifetime, operate at lower temperatures and use voltage derating.

faqs.question: Can Capxon radial capacitors be used in industrial applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Capxon radial capacitors are widely used in industrial controls and power supplies. Select capacitors with appropriate lifetime ratings for your application. KT series is recommended for industrial applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For industrial applications, select capacitors with 105°C rating and extended lifetime.

faqs.question: What is the ripple current rating of Capxon radial capacitors?
faqs.answer: Ripple current ratings range from 0.1A to 3.0A depending on capacitance, voltage, and case size. KM low-impedance series offers higher ripple current capability. When designing for ripple current, it is important to consider both the RMS value and the frequency spectrum. Higher frequencies may result in increased heating due to frequency-dependent ESR characteristics. Proper PCB layout with adequate copper area can help dissipate heat generated by ripple current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select capacitors with ripple current ratings at least 1.5x your application requirements.

faqs.question: Are Capxon radial capacitors available in low-profile versions?
faqs.answer: Yes, Capxon offers low-profile radial capacitors with reduced height for space-constrained applications. These are ideal for compact consumer electronics. For optimal performance and reliability, always follow the manufacturer's recommended operating conditions and application guidelines. Proper derating, thermal management, and regular monitoring are essential for achieving maximum capacitor lifetime in your specific application environment.
faqs.decisionGuide: For height-restricted applications, specify low-profile versions.

products.name: Radial Capacitor 1000uF 25V
products.shortDescription: Standard radial capacitor with 1000uF capacitance and 25V rating for general-purpose applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The KF-1000uF-25V is a standard radial aluminum electrolytic capacitor designed for general-purpose applications. With 1000uF capacitance and 25V rating, it provides reliable filtering and decoupling for low-voltage circuits.
This capacitor features a compact 10mm diameter case with 5mm lead spacing, making it ideal for space-constrained designs. The standard radial form factor enables easy through-hole mounting on PCBs.
Rated for 2000 hours at 85°C, this capacitor is suitable for consumer electronics and industrial applications with standard lifetime requirements.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: In my 15 years supporting power supply designs, I have consistently recommended the KF-1000uF-25V for industrial applications. The 1000uF/25V rating is perfect for 12V and 5V power supply output filtering. Capxon's Taiwan manufacturing quality ensures reliable performance. The compact 10mm size fits in most designs, and the 5mm lead spacing is compatible with standard PCB layouts.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent choice for low-voltage filtering applications

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications where 680uF is sufficient

comparison.impedance: Lower ESR for better high-frequency performance

applicationScenarios.description: Output filtering for 5V DC power supply

faqs.question: What is the maximum ripple current for the KF-1000uF-25V?
faqs.answer: The rated ripple current is 1.2A at 85°C and 120Hz. This rating ensures reliable operation in power supply applications with moderate switching frequencies. At lower temperatures, the capacitor can handle higher ripple currents - approximately 1.4A at 65°C. For high-frequency applications above 100kHz, the effective ripple current capability may be reduced due to increased ESR. Always verify the actual operating temperature and frequency conditions in your application to ensure the capacitor operates within its safe operating area.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications exceeding 1.2A ripple current, consider using multiple capacitors in parallel or upgrading to a higher capacitance model. Contact our FAE team for thermal modeling and ripple current calculations specific to your application.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of the KF-1000uF-25V at typical operating conditions?
faqs.answer: The rated lifetime is 2,000 hours at 85°C. Using the Arrhenius equation, we can calculate the expected lifetime at different operating temperatures. At 65°C, the expected lifetime extends to 8,000 hours. At 45°C, lifetime increases to 32,000 hours. At 25°C ambient, you can achieve up to 128,000 hours. Every 10°C reduction in operating temperature approximately doubles the capacitor lifetime. These calculations assume voltage derating to 80% of rated voltage and ripple current within specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For maximum capacitor lifetime, operate at the lowest practical temperature with 80% voltage derating. Contact our FAE team for lifetime calculations specific to your application conditions.

faqs.question: What voltage derating is recommended for reliable operation of the KF-1000uF-25V?
faqs.answer: Industry best practice recommends operating aluminum electrolytic capacitors at no more than 80% of their rated voltage. For this 25V rated capacitor, the maximum recommended operating voltage is 20V. This 20% derating significantly improves reliability and extends operational lifetime. For critical applications or high-temperature environments, 50% derating is recommended, limiting operating voltage to 13V. The voltage derating also provides margin for voltage transients and line regulation variations. Operating above 80% of rated voltage will significantly reduce capacitor lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your circuit to operate the capacitor at 80% or less of rated voltage for optimal reliability. Contact our FAE team for voltage derating recommendations specific to your application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the operating temperature range of the KF-1000uF-25V and how does it affect performance?
faqs.answer: This capacitor is rated for operation from -40°C to +85°C. At the maximum rated temperature of 85°C, the capacitor achieves its rated lifetime of 2,000 hours. As operating temperature decreases, capacitor lifetime increases exponentially following the Arrhenius relationship - approximately doubling for every 10°C reduction. Capacitance and ESR also vary with temperature, with capacitance typically increasing 10-15% at low temperatures and decreasing slightly at high temperatures. ESR generally decreases as temperature increases, improving high-frequency performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended capacitor lifetime, minimize operating temperature through proper thermal design and ventilation. Contact our FAE team for thermal management recommendations specific to your application.

faqs.question: What is the ESR of the KF-1000uF-25V and how does it affect circuit performance?
faqs.answer: The Equivalent Series Resistance (ESR) at 100Hz is approximately 0.15 Ohm. ESR represents the resistive losses in the capacitor and directly affects ripple voltage, power dissipation, and filtering effectiveness. Lower ESR results in lower ripple voltage for a given ripple current, reduced self-heating, and improved filtering at high frequencies. This capacitor's ESR characteristics make it suitable for switching power supplies and industrial controls. ESR typically decreases with increasing temperature and increases at higher frequencies.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring lower ESR, consider our KM low-impedance series or connect multiple capacitors in parallel. Contact our FAE team for ESR optimization recommendations.

faqs.question: Can multiple KF-1000uF-25V capacitors be connected in parallel for increased capability?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple capacitors can be connected in parallel to increase total capacitance and share ripple current. When connecting KF-1000uF-25V in parallel, the total capacitance adds linearly while ESR reduces proportionally. For example, two capacitors in parallel provide 2000uF capacitance and approximately 0.075 Ohm ESR (half the single capacitor ESR). This configuration also improves ripple current capability to 2.4A. Important considerations include: using identical part numbers for balanced current sharing, maintaining symmetrical PCB layout, ensuring adequate spacing for thermal management, and verifying the total inrush current doesn't exceed circuit limitations.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-current applications, consider parallel connection of multiple capacitors. Contact our FAE team for parallel capacitor bank design assistance and current sharing analysis.

products.name: Radial Capacitor 470uF 50V
products.shortDescription: Standard radial capacitor with 470uF capacitance and 50V rating for medium-voltage applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The KF-470uF-50V is a standard radial aluminum electrolytic capacitor designed for medium-voltage applications. With 470uF capacitance and 50V rating, it provides reliable filtering for 24V and 48V power supply circuits.
This capacitor features a 10mm diameter case with standard 5mm lead spacing. The optimized electrode design provides good ripple current capability.
Rated for 2000 hours at 85°C, this capacitor is suitable for industrial and consumer applications.

faeReview.author: David Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Electronics
faeReview.content: The KF-470uF-50V is ideal for 24V industrial power supplies. The 470uF value provides good filtering for medium-power applications. I frequently recommend this part for PLC power supplies and industrial control systems. The 50V rating provides good margin for 24V systems.

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications where 330uF is sufficient

alternativeParts.reason: Higher temperature rating for industrial use

applicationScenarios.description: Bulk capacitance for programmable logic controller

faqs.decisionGuide: For applications exceeding 0.9A ripple current, consider using multiple capacitors in parallel or upgrading to a higher capacitance model. Contact our FAE team for thermal modeling and ripple current calculations specific to your application.

faqs.answer: Industry best practice recommends operating aluminum electrolytic capacitors at no more than 80% of their rated voltage. For this 50V rated capacitor, the maximum recommended operating voltage is 40V. This 20% derating significantly improves reliability and extends operational lifetime. For critical applications or high-temperature environments, 50% derating is recommended, limiting operating voltage to 25V. The voltage derating also provides margin for voltage transients and line regulation variations. Operating above 80% of rated voltage will significantly reduce capacitor lifetime.

faqs.question: What is the operating temperature range of the KF-470uF-50V and how does it affect performance?
faqs.answer: This capacitor is rated for operation from -40°C to +85°C. At the maximum rated temperature of 85°C, the capacitor achieves its rated lifetime of 2,000 hours. As operating temperature decreases, capacitor lifetime increases exponentially following the Arrhenius relationship - approximately doubling for every 10°C reduction. Capacitance and ESR also vary with temperature, with capacitance typically increasing 10-15% at low temperatures and decreasing slightly at high temperatures. ESR generally decreases as temperature increases, improving high-frequency performance.

faqs.answer: The Equivalent Series Resistance (ESR) at 100Hz is approximately 0.20 Ohm. ESR represents the resistive losses in the capacitor and directly affects ripple voltage, power dissipation, and filtering effectiveness. Lower ESR results in lower ripple voltage for a given ripple current, reduced self-heating, and improved filtering at high frequencies. This capacitor's ESR characteristics make it suitable for switching power supplies and industrial controls. ESR typically decreases with increasing temperature and increases at higher frequencies.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring lower ESR, consider our KM low-impedance series or connect multiple capacitors in parallel. Contact our FAE team for ESR optimization recommendations.

faqs.question: Can multiple KF-470uF-50V capacitors be connected in parallel for increased capability?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple capacitors can be connected in parallel to increase total capacitance and share ripple current. When connecting KF-470uF-50V in parallel, the total capacitance adds linearly while ESR reduces proportionally. For example, two capacitors in parallel provide 940uF capacitance and approximately 0.10 Ohm ESR (half the single capacitor ESR). This configuration also improves ripple current capability to 1.8A. Important considerations include: using identical part numbers for balanced current sharing, maintaining symmetrical PCB layout, ensuring adequate spacing for thermal management, and verifying the total inrush current doesn't exceed circuit limitations.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-current applications, consider parallel connection of multiple capacitors. Contact our FAE team for parallel capacitor bank design assistance and current sharing analysis.

categories.name: Snap-in Capacitors
categories.description: High-capacitance snap-in aluminum electrolytic capacitors for power supply applications
categories.longDescription: Capxon snap-in capacitors are designed for high-power applications requiring large capacitance values and high ripple current capability. These capacitors feature PCB mounting terminals for secure mechanical attachment and excellent thermal performance. The snap-in series is ideal for industrial power supplies, inverters, motor drives, and UPS systems. Available in capacitance values from 1000uF to 68000uF and voltage ratings from 25V to 450VDC. Contact BeiLuo Electronics, your authorized Capxon distributor, for snap-in capacitor selection and technical support.
categories.selectionGuide: Select based on capacitance, voltage rating, ripple current requirements, and size constraints. SF series for standard, SM for low impedance, SH for high temperature.

faqs.question: What are the typical applications for Capxon snap-in capacitors?
faqs.answer: Snap-in capacitors are used in industrial power supplies, inverters, motor drives, UPS systems, and welding equipment. They are ideal for high-power applications requiring large capacitance values and high ripple current capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: For power supplies above 100W, snap-in capacitors provide better thermal performance and reliability.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SF, SM, and SH series?
faqs.answer: SF series is the standard snap-in capacitor for general applications. SM series features low impedance for high-frequency switching power supplies. SH series offers higher temperature ratings up to 105°C and longer lifetime for demanding environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SF series for standard applications. Use SM series for high-frequency power supplies. Use SH series for high-temperature applications.

faqs.question: What is the typical lifetime of Capxon snap-in capacitors?
faqs.answer: Standard snap-in capacitors are rated for 2,000 to 5,000 hours at 85°C or 105°C. High-temperature SH series offers up to 10,000 hours at 105°C. Lifetime can be extended significantly with proper derating.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended lifetime, operate at lower temperatures and use voltage derating.

faqs.question: What mounting options are available for snap-in capacitors?
faqs.answer: Snap-in capacitors feature PCB mounting terminals that snap into holes on the PCB. Standard spacing is 5mm or 7.5mm depending on case size. Some series also offer threaded terminals for additional security.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure PCB hole spacing matches the capacitor terminal spacing for proper mounting.

faqs.question: What is the ripple current capability of Capxon snap-in capacitors?
faqs.answer: Ripple current ratings range from 2A to 20A depending on capacitance, voltage, and case size. SM low-impedance series offers the highest ripple current capability for demanding applications. When designing for ripple current, it is important to consider both the RMS value and the frequency spectrum. Higher frequencies may result in increased heating due to frequency-dependent ESR characteristics. Proper PCB layout with adequate copper area can help dissipate heat generated by ripple current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select capacitors with ripple current ratings at least 1.5x your application requirements for reliable operation.

faqs.question: Can snap-in capacitors be used in parallel for higher capacitance?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple snap-in capacitors can be connected in parallel to increase total capacitance and share ripple current. This is common in high-power applications. Ensure balanced current sharing through symmetrical layout.
faqs.decisionGuide: For very high capacitance requirements, consider parallel connection of multiple snap-in capacitors.

products.name: Snap-in Capacitor 10000uF 63V
products.shortDescription: High-capacitance snap-in capacitor with 10000uF and 63V rating for industrial power supplies.
descriptionParagraphs: The SF-10000uF-63V is a high-capacitance snap-in aluminum electrolytic capacitor designed for industrial power supply applications. With 10000uF capacitance and 63V rating, it provides excellent filtering and energy storage for medium-voltage systems.
This capacitor features a 25mm diameter case with snap-in terminals for secure PCB mounting. The optimized electrode design provides high ripple current capability up to 5.2A.
Rated for 2000 hours at 85°C, this capacitor is suitable for industrial power supplies, inverters, and motor drives.

faeReview.author: Robert Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Industrial Power
faeReview.content: The SF-10000uF-63V is excellent for 48V industrial power supplies. The 10000uF provides substantial energy storage for hold-up time requirements. The 5.2A ripple current rating handles demanding loads well. I recommend this for any medium-power industrial application.

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications where 6800uF is sufficient

comparison.impedance: Lower ESR for better high-frequency performance

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for high-frequency switching power supplies

applicationScenarios.description: Output filtering for 48V industrial power supply
DC bus capacitance for motor drive inverter

faqs.question: What is the maximum ripple current for the SF-10000uF-63V?
faqs.answer: The rated ripple current is 5.2A at 85°C and 120Hz. This high rating makes it suitable for demanding industrial power supply applications. At lower temperatures, the capacitor can handle higher ripple currents - approximately 6.2A at 65°C. For high-frequency applications, the effective ripple current capability should be derated based on ESR characteristics.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications exceeding 5.2A ripple current, consider using multiple capacitors in parallel. Contact our FAE team for thermal analysis.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of the SF-10000uF-63V?
faqs.answer: The rated lifetime is 2,000 hours at 85°C. Using the Arrhenius equation, at 65°C the expected lifetime extends to 8,000 hours. At 45°C, lifetime increases to 32,000 hours. Every 10°C reduction approximately doubles the lifetime. For extended lifetime, operate with voltage derating and minimize temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: For maximum lifetime, operate at lower temperatures with 80% voltage derating. Contact our FAE team for specific calculations.

faqs.question: What mounting considerations are important for snap-in capacitors?
faqs.answer: Snap-in capacitors require proper PCB hole sizing for the snap-in terminals. Standard spacing is 7.5mm for this size. Ensure adequate clearance for the capacitor body and consider mechanical stress on the PCB. For high-vibration environments, additional mechanical support may be needed. Thermal management is also important - ensure adequate airflow or heatsinking for high ripple current applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify PCB layout compatibility and ensure proper thermal management for your application. Contact our FAE team for mounting recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the ESR of the SF-10000uF-63V?
faqs.answer: The Equivalent Series Resistance (ESR) at 100Hz is approximately 0.05 Ohm. This low ESR provides excellent filtering performance and minimizes self-heating under ripple current. ESR decreases with increasing temperature, improving performance at higher operating temperatures. For switching power supplies operating at higher frequencies, consider the frequency characteristics of ESR.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring even lower ESR, consider the SM low-impedance series. Contact our FAE team for ESR optimization.

faqs.question: Can the SF-10000uF-63V be used in series for higher voltage?
faqs.answer: While capacitors can be used in series for higher voltage ratings, this requires careful balancing to ensure equal voltage sharing. Each capacitor in series should have balancing resistors to prevent overvoltage on individual units. For most applications, it's better to select a single capacitor with the appropriate voltage rating. If series connection is necessary, consult our FAE team for proper design practices.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-voltage applications, select a single capacitor with appropriate voltage rating rather than series connection.

faqs.question: What are the physical dimensions of the SF-10000uF-63V?
faqs.answer: The SF-10000uF-63V has a diameter of 25mm and length of 40mm. The snap-in terminals have 7.5mm spacing. These dimensions are important for PCB layout and mechanical design. Ensure adequate clearance around the capacitor for airflow and servicing. The capacitor should be mounted with the terminals inserted fully into the PCB holes until the snap tabs engage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify mechanical fit in your enclosure and PCB layout before finalizing the design.

This capacitor features a 25mm diameter case with snap-in terminals for secure mounting. The design provides good ripple current capability and reliable performance.
Rated for 2000 hours at 85°C, this capacitor is suitable for industrial power supplies and motor drives.

faqs.question: What is the maximum ripple current for the SF-4700uF-100V?
faqs.answer: The rated ripple current is 4.5A at 85°C and 120Hz. This rating provides reliable operation in high-voltage industrial applications. At lower temperatures, the capacitor can handle higher ripple currents - approximately 5.4A at 65°C. For high-frequency applications, derating may be necessary based on ESR characteristics.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications exceeding 4.5A ripple current, consider parallel connection. Contact our FAE team for thermal analysis.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of the SF-4700uF-100V?
faqs.answer: The rated lifetime is 2,000 hours at 85°C. Using the Arrhenius equation, at 65°C the expected lifetime extends to 8,000 hours. At 45°C, lifetime increases to 32,000 hours. Every 10°C reduction approximately doubles the lifetime. Voltage derating also significantly extends lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: For maximum lifetime, operate at lower temperatures with 80% voltage derating.

faqs.question: What voltage derating is recommended for the SF-4700uF-100V?
faqs.answer: For this 100V rated capacitor, the maximum recommended operating voltage is 80V (80% derating). This provides good reliability margin for industrial applications. For critical applications, 50% derating to 50V provides even better reliability. The derating also provides margin for voltage transients common in industrial environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for 80% voltage derating for good reliability. Use 50% derating for critical applications.

faqs.question: What is the ESR of the SF-4700uF-100V?
faqs.answer: The Equivalent Series Resistance (ESR) at 100Hz is approximately 0.08 Ohm. This provides good filtering performance for high-voltage applications. The ESR characteristics are optimized for industrial power supply frequencies. ESR decreases with temperature, improving performance at higher operating temperatures.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring lower ESR, consider the SM series or parallel connection.

faqs.question: What are the mounting requirements for the SF-4700uF-100V?
faqs.answer: This capacitor requires 7.5mm terminal spacing on the PCB. The 25mm diameter and 50mm length require adequate clearance in the enclosure. Ensure proper airflow for thermal management, especially when operating at high ripple currents. The snap-in terminals should fully engage with the PCB for secure mounting.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify PCB layout and enclosure fit before finalizing design. Ensure adequate thermal management.

faqs.question: Can the SF-4700uF-100V be used in parallel with other capacitors?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple capacitors can be connected in parallel to increase capacitance and share ripple current. This is common in high-power applications. Two capacitors in parallel provide 9400uF and can share the ripple current load. Ensure symmetrical layout for balanced current sharing.
faqs.decisionGuide: For very high capacitance or current requirements, consider parallel connection.

categories.name: SMD Capacitors
categories.description: Surface mount aluminum electrolytic capacitors for compact designs
categories.longDescription: Capxon SMD aluminum electrolytic capacitors are designed for compact electronic devices where space is at a premium. These surface mount capacitors offer reliable performance in a small package, making them ideal for consumer electronics, portable devices, and high-density PCBs. Available in various case sizes and voltage ratings, SMD capacitors provide an excellent solution for space-constrained designs. Contact BeiLuo Electronics for SMD capacitor selection guidance.
categories.selectionGuide: Select based on capacitance, voltage rating, and case size constraints. VF series for standard, VM for low impedance.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of SMD capacitors?
faqs.answer: SMD capacitors offer several advantages: smaller size for compact designs, automated assembly compatibility, reduced PCB space requirements, and lower profile for thin devices. They are ideal for consumer electronics and portable devices where space is limited.
faqs.decisionGuide: For space-constrained designs, SMD capacitors provide the best solution.

faqs.question: What case sizes are available for Capxon SMD capacitors?
faqs.answer: Capxon SMD capacitors are available in standard case sizes including 4x5.4mm, 5x5.4mm, 6.3x5.4mm, 8x6.2mm, 8x10.2mm, and 10x10.2mm. Larger case sizes offer higher capacitance and voltage ratings. For optimal performance and reliability, always follow the manufacturer's recommended operating conditions and application guidelines. Proper derating, thermal management, and regular monitoring are essential for achieving maximum capacitor lifetime in your specific application environment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select case size based on capacitance requirements and available PCB space.

faqs.question: What is the typical lifetime of SMD capacitors?
faqs.answer: Capxon SMD capacitors are typically rated for 1000 to 2000 hours at 85°C or 105°C. The compact size results in slightly lower lifetime compared to through-hole types, but proper derating can extend operational life significantly.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended lifetime, operate at lower temperatures and voltages.

faqs.question: Can SMD capacitors handle high ripple current?
faqs.answer: SMD capacitors have lower ripple current capability compared to larger through-hole types due to their smaller size. However, VM low-impedance series offers improved ripple current handling for demanding applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high ripple current applications, select VM series or use multiple capacitors in parallel.

faqs.question: What reflow soldering profile is recommended?
faqs.answer: Capxon SMD capacitors are compatible with standard lead-free reflow profiles with peak temperatures up to 260°C. Follow the manufacturer's recommended thermal profile to avoid damage. Preheating is important to prevent thermal shock.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow Capxon's recommended reflow profile for reliable soldering.

faqs.question: Are SMD capacitors suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Standard SMD capacitors are suitable for consumer and industrial applications. For automotive applications requiring AEC-Q200 qualification, select Capxon's automotive-grade SMD series which undergo additional testing and qualification.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, select AEC-Q200 qualified automotive series.

products.name: SMD Capacitor 100uF 16V
products.shortDescription: Compact SMD capacitor with 100uF and 16V rating for consumer electronics. Designed for reliable performance in demanding applications with Taiwan manufacturing quality.
descriptionParagraphs: The VF-100uF-16V is a compact SMD aluminum electrolytic capacitor designed for consumer electronics applications. With 100uF capacitance and 16V rating in a small 6.3x5.4mm package, it provides reliable decoupling and filtering for low-voltage circuits.
This capacitor features surface mount terminals for automated assembly. The compact design is ideal for space-constrained PCBs in portable devices.
Rated for 1000 hours at 85°C, this capacitor is suitable for consumer electronics with standard lifetime requirements.

faeReview.author: Lisa Chen
faeReview.title: FAE - Consumer Electronics
faeReview.content: The VF-100uF-16V is perfect for small consumer electronics. The compact 6.3mm size fits well in portable devices. I use this frequently in USB-powered devices and small gadgets. The 100uF provides good decoupling for 5V and 3.3V rails.

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications requiring lower ESR

faqs.question: What is the maximum ripple current for the VF-100uF-16V?
faqs.answer: The rated ripple current is 0.15A at 85°C and 100kHz. This rating is suitable for low-power consumer electronics applications. The compact size limits ripple current capability compared to larger capacitors. For higher ripple current requirements, consider using multiple capacitors in parallel or selecting the VM low-impedance series.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications with higher ripple current, use multiple capacitors in parallel or select VM series.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of the VF-100uF-16V?
faqs.answer: The rated lifetime is 1,000 hours at 85°C. Using the Arrhenius equation, at 65°C the expected lifetime extends to 4,000 hours. At 45°C, lifetime increases to 16,000 hours. Every 10°C reduction approximately doubles the lifetime. The compact size results in slightly lower base lifetime compared to larger capacitors.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended lifetime, operate at lower temperatures and use voltage derating.

faqs.question: What is the recommended soldering profile for the VF-100uF-16V?
faqs.answer: This SMD capacitor is compatible with standard lead-free reflow profiles with peak temperatures up to 260°C. Recommended profile: preheat to 150-180°C, soak for 60-120 seconds, reflow peak at 245-260°C for 30-60 seconds. Avoid exceeding 260°C peak temperature. Allow adequate cooling time after reflow.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow standard lead-free reflow profiles. Avoid thermal shock during soldering.

faqs.question: What PCB layout considerations are important for SMD capacitors?
faqs.answer: Ensure proper pad sizing according to the capacitor specifications. Maintain adequate spacing from other components for thermal management. Place capacitors close to the ICs they are decoupling to minimize trace inductance. Use adequate copper pour for heat dissipation if operating at high ripple currents.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow recommended PCB layout guidelines for optimal performance and reliability.

faqs.question: Can the VF-100uF-16V be used in parallel for higher capacitance?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple SMD capacitors can be connected in parallel to increase total capacitance. This is common practice for decoupling applications. Two capacitors in parallel provide 200uF and can share the load. Maintain short, symmetrical traces for balanced current sharing.
faqs.decisionGuide: For higher capacitance requirements, use multiple capacitors in parallel with symmetrical layout.

faqs.question: What is the ESR of the VF-100uF-16V?
faqs.answer: The Equivalent Series Resistance (ESR) at 100kHz is approximately 1.5 Ohm. This ESR level is typical for compact SMD capacitors and provides adequate filtering for consumer electronics applications. The VM series offers lower ESR for applications requiring better high-frequency performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring lower ESR, consider the VM low-impedance series.

products.name: SMD Capacitor 220uF 25V
products.shortDescription: Compact SMD capacitor with 220uF and 25V rating for higher voltage applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The VF-220uF-25V is a compact SMD aluminum electrolytic capacitor designed for applications requiring higher voltage handling. With 220uF capacitance and 25V rating in an 8x6.2mm package, it provides reliable filtering for medium-voltage circuits.
This capacitor features surface mount terminals for automated assembly. The 8mm case size provides higher capacitance while maintaining compact dimensions.
Rated for 1000 hours at 85°C, this capacitor is suitable for consumer and industrial electronics.

faeReview.author: Kevin Wu
faeReview.title: FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The VF-220uF-25V provides good capacitance in a compact SMD package. The 25V rating is useful for 12V and 24V systems. I recommend this for industrial control boards where space is limited but higher voltage handling is needed.
faeReview.highlight: Good capacitance for compact industrial applications

alternativeParts.useCase: Use when smaller size is needed
Use for applications requiring lower ESR

companionParts.description: SMD capacitor for lower voltage rails

applicationScenarios.description: Filtering for 12V power supply in compact design

faqs.question: What is the maximum ripple current for the VF-220uF-25V?
faqs.answer: The rated ripple current is 0.25A at 85°C and 100kHz. This provides adequate ripple handling for medium-power applications. The larger 8mm case size allows higher ripple current compared to smaller SMD capacitors.
faqs.decisionGuide: For higher ripple current requirements, use multiple capacitors in parallel or select VM series.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of the VF-220uF-25V?
faqs.answer: The rated lifetime is 1,000 hours at 85°C. Using the Arrhenius equation, at 65°C the expected lifetime extends to 4,000 hours. At 45°C, lifetime increases to 16,000 hours. Proper derating significantly extends operational life.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended lifetime, operate at lower temperatures with voltage derating.

faqs.question: What is the recommended soldering profile?
faqs.answer: Use standard lead-free reflow profile with peak temperature up to 260°C. Preheat to 150-180°C, soak for 60-120 seconds, reflow peak at 245-260°C for 30-60 seconds. Allow proper cooling after reflow to avoid thermal shock.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow standard lead-free reflow procedures for reliable soldering.

faqs.question: What are the PCB layout recommendations?
faqs.answer: Use proper pad sizes as specified in the datasheet. Place capacitors close to load devices for effective decoupling. Maintain adequate spacing for thermal management. Use copper pours for heat dissipation when operating at higher ripple currents.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow recommended PCB layout guidelines for optimal performance.

faqs.question: Can this capacitor be used in parallel?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple capacitors can be paralleled for higher capacitance. Two capacitors provide 440uF total. Use symmetrical layout for balanced current sharing. This is useful for applications requiring higher capacitance in limited space.
faqs.decisionGuide: For higher capacitance, parallel multiple capacitors with proper layout.

faqs.question: What is the ESR specification?
faqs.answer: The ESR at 100kHz is approximately 1.0 Ohm. This provides good filtering performance for most applications. The VM series offers lower ESR for applications requiring better high-frequency characteristics.
faqs.decisionGuide: For lower ESR requirements, consider the VM low-impedance series.

categories.name: Automotive Capacitors
categories.description: AEC-Q200 qualified automotive-grade aluminum electrolytic capacitors
categories.longDescription: Capxon automotive-grade capacitors are specifically designed and qualified to meet AEC-Q200 standards for automotive applications. These capacitors undergo rigorous testing including temperature cycling, vibration, and humidity resistance to ensure reliable performance in vehicle environments. With extended temperature ratings and enhanced reliability, automotive capacitors are ideal for EV charging systems, onboard electronics, LED lighting, and engine control units. Contact BeiLuo Electronics for automotive capacitor selection and technical support.
categories.selectionGuide: Select based on automotive application requirements, temperature range, and AEC-Q200 qualification level.

faqs.question: What is AEC-Q200 qualification?
faqs.answer: AEC-Q200 is the global standard for passive electronic components used in automotive applications. It defines rigorous testing requirements including temperature cycling, vibration, humidity, and mechanical stress tests. Capxon automotive capacitors meet these stringent requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, always select AEC-Q200 qualified capacitors.

faqs.question: What temperature ranges are available for automotive capacitors?
faqs.answer: Capxon automotive capacitors are available with temperature ratings up to 125°C or 150°C, significantly higher than standard industrial capacitors. This extended temperature range is essential for under-hood applications and EV systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select temperature rating based on the maximum operating temperature in your automotive application.

faqs.question: What applications are suitable for automotive capacitors?
faqs.answer: Automotive capacitors are used in EV charging systems, onboard electronics, LED lighting, engine control units, transmission control, and ADAS systems. Any application requiring AEC-Q200 qualification and high reliability in vehicle environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: For any automotive electronics application, use AEC-Q200 qualified automotive capacitors.

faqs.question: What is the typical lifetime of automotive capacitors?
faqs.answer: Automotive capacitors are rated for 2000 to 5000 hours at maximum temperature. The extended temperature rating and robust construction provide reliable performance over the vehicle lifetime. Actual field lifetime depends on operating conditions and derating.
faqs.decisionGuide: For maximum reliability, apply appropriate voltage and temperature derating.

faqs.question: Are automotive capacitors more expensive than standard capacitors?
faqs.answer: Yes, automotive capacitors typically cost 20-50% more than standard industrial capacitors due to additional testing, qualification, and enhanced construction. However, the cost is justified by the higher reliability and qualification to automotive standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, the additional cost of AEC-Q200 qualified capacitors is necessary for reliability.

faqs.question: What documentation is available for automotive capacitors?
faqs.answer: Capxon provides PPAP documentation, AEC-Q200 test reports, material declarations, and reliability data for automotive capacitors. BeiLuo Electronics can provide all necessary documentation for automotive qualification and compliance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for automotive documentation and PPAP support.

products.name: Automotive Capacitor 100uF 50V
products.shortDescription: AEC-Q200 qualified automotive capacitor with 100uF and 50V rating for vehicle electronics.
descriptionParagraphs: The AF-100uF-50V is an AEC-Q200 qualified automotive aluminum electrolytic capacitor designed for vehicle electronics applications. With 100uF capacitance and 50V rating, it provides reliable filtering for automotive systems.

This capacitor meets stringent AEC-Q200 requirements including extended temperature range up to 125°C, vibration resistance, and humidity testing. The robust construction ensures reliable performance in vehicle environments.
Rated for 2000 hours at 125°C, this capacitor is suitable for automotive applications including EV charging, LED lighting, and onboard electronics.

faeReview.author: Thomas Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive Electronics
faeReview.content: The AF-100uF-50V is an excellent choice for automotive LED lighting applications. The AEC-Q200 qualification and 125°C rating provide the reliability needed for vehicle environments. I have successfully used this in multiple automotive projects with excellent results.
faeReview.highlight: Reliable AEC-Q200 qualified capacitor for automotive applications

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications where 68uF is sufficient

applicationScenarios.description: Filtering for automotive LED lighting system

faqs.question: What AEC-Q200 tests does the AF-100uF-50V pass?
faqs.answer: The AF-100uF-50V passes all AEC-Q200 required tests including: high temperature storage, temperature cycling (-40°C to +125°C), vibration resistance, mechanical shock, humidity resistance, and solderability. These rigorous tests ensure reliable performance in automotive environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, always verify AEC-Q200 qualification status.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime in automotive applications?
faqs.answer: The rated lifetime is 2,000 hours at 125°C. In typical automotive applications with proper derating, actual field lifetime can exceed 10 years. The Arrhenius relationship applies - every 10°C reduction approximately doubles the lifetime. Most automotive applications operate well below the maximum rated temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: For maximum reliability, operate with voltage derating and minimize operating temperature.

faqs.question: What voltage derating is recommended for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: For automotive applications, industry best practice recommends 50% voltage derating for maximum reliability. For this 50V rated capacitor, limit operating voltage to 25V. This conservative derating accounts for voltage transients, load dumps, and the critical nature of automotive systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 50% voltage derating for automotive applications to ensure maximum reliability.

faqs.question: Can this capacitor be used in engine compartment applications?
faqs.answer: The AF-100uF-50V with 125°C rating is suitable for many engine compartment applications. However, for extreme under-hood temperatures, consider the AH series with 150°C rating. Always verify the maximum expected temperature in the specific mounting location.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extreme temperature locations, select AH series with 150°C rating.

faqs.question: What documentation is available for automotive qualification?
faqs.answer: Complete PPAP documentation is available including: design records, material certifications, AEC-Q200 test reports, process flow diagrams, PFMEA, control plan, measurement system analysis, dimensional results, material performance test results, initial process studies, qualified laboratory documentation, appearance approval report, sample production parts, master sample, checking aids, and customer-specific requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for complete PPAP documentation package.

faqs.question: What is the ESR specification for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: The ESR at 100kHz is approximately 0.8 Ohm. This provides good filtering performance for automotive electronics. The ESR is optimized for the switching frequencies typical in automotive DC-DC converters and LED drivers. Lower ESR reduces self-heating and improves efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring lower ESR, contact our FAE team for recommendations.

products.name: Automotive Capacitor 470uF 63V
products.shortDescription: High-temperature automotive capacitor with 470uF and 63V rating for demanding vehicle applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The AH-470uF-63V is a high-temperature AEC-Q200 qualified automotive aluminum electrolytic capacitor. With 470uF capacitance and 63V rating, it provides substantial energy storage for automotive power systems.
This capacitor features an extended 150°C temperature rating, making it suitable for extreme under-hood applications. The AEC-Q200 qualification ensures reliable performance in vehicle environments.
Rated for 2000 hours at 150°C, this capacitor is ideal for EV charging systems, engine control units, and other high-temperature automotive applications.

faeReview.author: Michael Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive Power
faeReview.content: The AH-470uF-63V is exceptional for high-temperature automotive applications. The 150°C rating handles the most demanding under-hood environments. I have used this in EV charging systems with excellent reliability. The 470uF provides good energy storage for power hold-up requirements.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent for extreme temperature automotive applications

alternativeParts.useCase: Use for applications where 330uF is sufficient

alternativeParts.reason: Lower temperature rating for cost savings
alternativeParts.useCase: Use for less demanding temperature environments

applicationScenarios.description: Energy storage for EV DC-DC converter
Power filtering for engine control module

faqs.question: What makes the AH series suitable for extreme temperatures?
faqs.answer: The AH series uses advanced electrolyte formulations and construction techniques that enable operation up to 150°C. This includes high-temperature electrolyte, improved sealing, and enhanced electrode materials. These enhancements allow reliable operation in the most demanding automotive environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extreme temperature applications, the AH series provides the highest reliability.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime at 150°C?
faqs.answer: The rated lifetime is 2,000 hours at 150°C. While this may seem short compared to lower temperature ratings, it represents excellent performance for such extreme conditions. In actual automotive use, operating temperatures are typically much lower, resulting in significantly extended field life.
faqs.decisionGuide: The 150°C rating provides safety margin for extreme conditions while actual life is much longer at typical operating temperatures.

faqs.question: What applications require 150°C rated capacitors?
faqs.answer: 150°C rated capacitors are needed for extreme under-hood applications, EV charging systems, engine control units, transmission control modules, and other locations where temperatures can reach very high levels. Standard 125°C capacitors may not survive in these extreme environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 150°C capacitors for extreme temperature locations where 125°C ratings are insufficient.

faqs.question: What is the ripple current capability at high temperatures?
faqs.answer: The rated ripple current is 1.2A at 150°C and 100kHz. This is impressive considering the extreme temperature. The ripple current capability increases at lower temperatures - approximately 1.7A at 125°C and 2.4A at 105°C.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high ripple current at extreme temperatures, ensure adequate thermal management.

faqs.question: What voltage derating is recommended for 150°C operation?
faqs.answer: For 150°C operation, conservative voltage derating is essential. Recommend 50% derating for this 63V capacitor - limit operating voltage to 32V. This provides margin for voltage transients and ensures reliable operation at extreme temperatures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 50% voltage derating for 150°C operation to ensure maximum reliability.

faqs.question: Is PPAP documentation available for the AH series?
faqs.answer: Yes, complete PPAP Level 3 documentation is available for AH series automotive capacitors. This includes all required documentation for automotive supplier qualification: design records, material certifications, AEC-Q200 test reports, process documentation, and ongoing reliability data.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for complete PPAP documentation for AH series capacitors.
